message:
refactor(observability): rename Deadlocked to Fragmented, treat edge-free graphs as healthy

The HealthClassification::Deadlocked variant was misleading —
a disconnected spectral graph means the dependency topology has
fragmented into islands, not that a trapped wait-cycle deadlock
exists.  Actual deadlock detection is handled by the SCC path in
the scheduler, not by spectral connectivity.

Changes:
- Deadlocked renamed to Fragmented with updated doc comments
  explaining it is a structural fragmentation signal.
- Edge-free graphs (zero edges) now return Healthy{margin:0.0}
  instead of being classified as fragmented — isolated nodes
  with no dependency structure cannot fragment.
- Module-level docs updated to remove deadlock terminology.
- All tests and Display impls updated to use Fragmented.

New test: classify_edge_free_graph_as_healthy verifies 3 isolated
nodes are classified as Healthy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
